source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (General)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.


ABOUT YOU

Engineering at Outtake is built on ownership, clarity, and end-to-end impact. We hire entrepreneurial generalists who think in systems, not tickets — people who want to design architecture, ship fast, and solve problems that span the entire stack.

Our teams are flat and autonomous: engineers ship fast, make product decisions, and own outcomes from concept to deployment. We optimize for grit + velocity, which means every engineer operates with the scope, influence, and equity of a future CTO.

Our goal is to bring on engineers who will grow with Outtake's trajectory - and because of the responsibility, autonomy and talent density here.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

When the life-support systems on your Mars habitat begin to fail, are you the engineer the team trusts to hack together a solution in hours?

No worries, neither are we - but we DO want you to be the type of engineer who is unafraid to dive into any part of the codebase to accomplish the mission.

Often getting the job done involves building functional v0s that eventually evolve into scalable production systems. Can you navigate this journey effectively?

Do you have well reasoned opinions that rapidly update to new information - whether from customers, peers, or the environment?




WHAT YOU’LL ACHIEVE

 - Contribute to the whole platform - whether it’s in the BE where you tinker with agent framework that achieves all complex tasks or the FE where you make our incredibly powerful workflows simple & intuitive.

 - Partner with customers who are some of the most socially meaningful individuals on the planet - these brands, reputations, and names move billions daily.

 - Work with an elite team with high success track record at prior companies & become a better version of yourself in the process.

 - Learn the ability to carry zen into any situation of chaos as you continuously overcome obstacles and fully grow into your abilities.

 - Become a leader as Outtake grows and define the culture of a high impact organization.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BRING

 - Proven track record of execution: 2+ years experience as software engineer building world class products.

 - Thoughtful problem solver: You approach problems holistically, starting with a clear and accurate understanding of the context. You think critically about the implications of your decisions, and are able to consider both the business and the technical tradeoffs of your solutions.

 - Not ideological about technology: To you, technologies are about tradeoffs. You may be opinionated, but you’re not ideological, can learn new technologies and are receptive to new ideas.

 - Organized and self-sufficient: You are comfortable managing yourself, navigate ambiguity and finding ways to create impact.

 - Owner of your mission: You are a owner of the products that you create, meaning you take pride in the quality, maintenance and understandability of the products you build and the code you write.
   
   


BONUS POINTS IF…

 - You have experience working in a high growth early stage startup.

 - You have experience building agents, or fine-t

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.

About You

Outtake is looking for a technical product champion who bridges the gap between our customers' security challenges and our AI-powered platform to join our team as a Sales Engineer. We're seeking someone who thrives at the intersection of sales and engineering, with the technical fluency to navigate complex cybersecurity environments and the communication skills to command the room with CISOs and security leaders.

This person will be instrumental in supporting our most complex enterprise deals — leading technical discovery, delivering compelling demos, and designing solutions that map Outtake's platform to real-world threat landscapes. The right candidate has an insatiable curiosity about cybersecurity, genuinely enjoys solving hard problems alongside customers, and gets energized by helping close deals.

What You'll Need to Bring

 - 2–6 years of experience in solutions engineering, sales engineering, or technical pre-sales, ideally in cybersecurity or adjacent infrastructure/SaaS

 - Strong understanding of modern attack surfaces, threat detection, and security operations workflows

 - Technical fluency with APIs, data models, and system architecture — you can speak credibly to both engineering teams and security practitioners

 - Exceptional communication and presentation skills across technical and executive audiences

 - Natural curiosity and genuine passion for cybersecurity and continuous learning

 - Highly adaptable with the ability to context-switch between customer conversations, live demos, and technical deep-dives

What You'll Achieve

 - Partner with Sales to lead technical discovery and solution design for complex, high-value enterprise deals

 - Deliver compelling demos tailored to each prospect's security environment and threat landscape

 - Architect proof-of-concept deployments that showcase Outtake's agentic AI capabilities

 - Serve as the technical authority in sales cycles, fielding deep questions on threat classification, automated response, and platform architecture

 - Collaborate with Product and Engineering to translate field insights into roadmap priorities

What Success Looks Like

 - Become the trusted technical advisor that prospects rely on throughout the sales process

 - Consistently contribute to deal velocity and win rates through strong technical positioning

 - Build repeatable demo workflows and technical collateral that scale the sales motion

 - Surface patterns in prospect needs and competitive intelligence that shape product strategy

 - Seamlessly hand off closed deals to post-sale teams with thorough implementation context

Bonus Points

 - Background in cybersecurity — whether in security operations, threat intelligence, or selling to security buyers

 - Familiarity with modern AI/ML concepts, particularly as applied to threat detection and response

 - SQL or similar analytical/technical skills

 - Experience at a fast-paced startup



LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Col

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.

Outtake isn’t a place for order-takers. It’s a place for enterprise sellers who think like builders—who love crafting value, navigating ambiguity, and convincing executives to bet on a new category. Our product solves a problem with universal demand and immediate ROI, which means you’ll have the chance to create and close meaningful business quickly.

We’re looking for ambitious individuals who can navigate multiple decision-makers at the highest levels, creatively engage new prospects, and consistently exceed targets. You’ll be part of a nimble team with a bias toward action, building new relationships with global enterprise organizations.




WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BRING

 - Proven track record of closing six-figure B2B SaaS deals at the enterprise level, including experience selling to multiple stakeholders and aligning with their business goals.

 - Creative hunter mindset: You don’t wait for leads to show up; you proactively reach out, experiment with new tactics, and push forward—even in the face of no.

 - Technical fluency: You’re comfortable discussing relevant product features and integrations with highly technical buyers (CISOs, Threat Intel teams, etc.), and can translate complex concepts into business value.




WHAT YOU’LL ACHIEVE

 - Build a new category in the threat intelligence and brand risk space, working hand-in-hand with the product team to refine and capitalize on strong market fit.

 - Partner with the biggest names in the world—CISOs, Threat Intelligence leaders, and brand owners who handle crucial security and PR incidents daily.

 - Champion Outtake’s solutions through high-impact product demos and strategic sales engagements that showcase how we can transform incident response and brand protection on a global scale.

 - Collaborate with a world-class team of product experts, engineers, and go-to-market professionals to continually evolve sales strategy and processes.

 - Maintain a relentless focus on growth by continuously improving our outbound strategy, refining messaging, and experimenting with new ways to break into untapped verticals.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Consistently exceeding monthly and quarterly revenue targets, with a strong pipeline of enterprise opportunities.

 - Streamlining and iterating on the enterprise sales cycle to deliver a high-quality buyer experience from first touch to close.

 - Providing product feedback that directly impacts the roadmap, ensuring Outtake remains the premier incident management and brand protection platform.

 - Becoming an indispensable resource for our customers—someone who fosters trust, builds strong relationships, and is the go-to point of contact for strategic escalations and long-term success.





LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Collaboration, speed, and clarity matter.

 - Health: 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision for employees.

 - Time Away: Flexible PTO. We trust adults to manage energy, not clock time.

 - Culture & Team: An

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (SDR)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.

We’re looking for an ambitious SDR who wants to grow fast—someone who can break into accounts, create signal in a noisy market, and fuel our sales motion. You’ll earn trust quickly and graduate into closing roles as you demonstrate impact.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED TO BRING:

 - Proven success in sales —or, if earlier in your career, a clear track record of grit, curiosity, and drive in a competitive environment.

 - Highly motivated, action-oriented, and unfazed by rejection—you see outreach as a craft and love finding creative ways to break through

 - Strong written and verbal communication—you know how to be concise, persuasive, and thoughtful in every interaction

 - Analytical and adaptable—you test new approaches constantly and are always looking for a better way to do things

 - Comfortable with ambiguity—you’re excited to build the plane as we fly it




WHAT YOU'LL ACHIEVE

 - Joining a fast-growing VC-backed startup solving a critical problem with clear market pull, but massive white space still ahead

 - Partnering closely with our leadership team to shape our outbound motion, from target accounts to messaging and playbooks

 - Reaching out to CISOs, Threat Intel leads, and legal teams at some of the most recognizable brands in the world—via email, phone, LinkedIn, conferences, and creative campaigns

 - Becoming an expert on digital impersonation scams, domain abuse, and takedown strategy—serving as a trusted first point of contact for Fortune 500 security teams

 - Laying the foundation for a career in enterprise sales, with a clear path to Account Executive and leadership roles as we scale



LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Collaboration, speed, and clarity matter.

 - Health: 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision for employees.

 - Time Away: Flexible PTO. We trust adults to manage energy, not clock time.

 - Culture & Team: Annual company retreats and regular in-person events.

Outtake is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive team.

source_jobs.title: Deployment Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.


ABOUT YOU

Our Deployment team is the connective tissue between Sales, Engineering, and our customers. We make sure pilots run smoothly, product performance is flawless, and customer insights drive the roadmap.

The Deployment Associate role is an entry point into that work. You’ll support and learn from our senior Deployment team while building a 360° view of our product, customers, and data. Your job is to keep eyes on every corner of the product, spot trends and anomalies before customers do, and bring structured insights back to the team so we can stay two steps ahead.

Over time (think months, not years), you’ll grow into owning smaller accounts and driving more direct customer interactions. But from day one, your impact will come from your ability to notice what others miss, connect dots across data points, and help us deliver excellence through care, attention, and product feedback.




WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BRING

 - Curiosity and persistence: you dig into “why” until you understand the root cause.

 - Attention to detail: you notice when something doesn’t look right — and you verify before sounding the alarm.

 - Eagerness to learn: you want to understand both the technical side of the product and the human side of our customer relationships.

 - Clear communication: you can write up observations, explain them concisely, and highlight their impact.

 - Team-first mindset: you’re here to help the team move faster, anticipate needs, and deliver excellence.




WHAT YOU'LL ACHIEVE

 - Shadow and support senior Deployment leads on customer pilots, onboardings, and post-sales care, learning our workflows end to end.

 - Proactively monitor product performance across channels (TikTok, X, Instagram, Bluesky, YouTube, etc.) to catch issues before customers do.

 - Run regular product health checks: validate agent decisions, spot anomalies, track pipeline flow, and flag unexpected changes.

 - Connect dots between what you see in the product, what you hear from customers, and where the engineering team can improve the experience.

 - Document and share insights in a way that helps the team act quickly — whether it’s a pattern in missed detections or a UI change that would save customers time.

 - Participate in internal product reviews and triage sessions, learning how deployment feedback shapes engineering priorities.

 - Think ahead: suggest checks, tests, or improvements that keep us proactive instead of reactive.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - You anticipate and address potential issues early, helping the Deployment team operate smoothly and avoid last-minute disruptions.

 - You surface insights that influence product decisions or customer outcomes.

 - You develop a strong working knowledge of our product and deployment process, setting you up to own small accounts after a few months.

 - Customers indirectly benefit from your work because the product feels polished, reliable, and ahead of their needs.




BONUS POINTS

 - Exposure to SaaS or software products.

 - Basic SQL or comfort learning lightweight data tools.

 - Familiarity with social platforms or

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.

Don’t see the perfect role but want to be part of Outtake? We’re always meeting exceptional people. If you’re sharp, hungry, and excited about building AI security with a small, dense team, introduce yourself. Tell us what you’re great at and how you can help us build the trust layer of the internet—we hire based on impact, not just openings.



LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Collaboration, speed, and clarity matter.

 - Health: 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision for employees.

source_jobs.title: Technical Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Outtake is looking for a Technical Account Manager to lead customer-facing engagements and ensure successful delivery from pilot to long-term adoption. In this role, you’ll live at the intersection of customer needs, product capabilities, and internal execution. If you’re customer-obsessed, like building trusted relationships, and are excited to roll up your sleeves technically, this role is for you.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Pre-sales execution: Partner with sales team to understand customer goals, gather technical requirements, support pilots, and deliver tailored demos

 - Platform configuration: Own customer environment deployment and post-sale technical execution, including platform spin-up, prompt engineering, and configuration

 - Technical POC: Serve as the primary technical point of contact for customers, managing expectations and guiding them through product capabilities and evolution

 - Customer delight: Lead customer onboarding and enablement to drive adoption and monitor health signals to proactively identify risks, new revenue opportunities, and next steps

 - Champion for scale: Capture and share customer insights, best practices, and recurring patterns to educate internal teams and improve product and process scalability


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BRING

 - 4+ years of experience in customer-facing technical roles such as account management, solutions engineering, or customer success

 - Strong technical aptitude with the ability to deploy, configure, and troubleshoot platforms

 - Experience working directly with enterprise customers, including a variety of technical and non-technical stakeholders

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly and confidently

 - Highly organized and capable of managing multiple customer engagements at once

 - Natural curiosity and genuine passion ****for product excellence and continuous improvement

 - Willingness for occasional (~1-2x quarterly) travel to customer sites for check-ins and relationship-building


BONUS POINTS

 - Hands-on agent experience: Exposure to setting up and configuring AI agents

 - Experience at a fast-paced start-up: You’ve already cut your teeth at an early-stage start-up or other high growth environment

 - Project implementation experience: Not only have you designed a program, but seen it through to fruition with detailed planning, execution, and outcome-tracking



LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Collaboration, speed, and clarity matter.

 - Health: 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision for employees.

 - Time Away: Flexible PTO. We trust adults to manage energy, not clock time.

 - Culture & Team: Annual company retreats and regular in-person events.

Outtake is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive team.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (Product)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.


THE ROLE

This is a product engineering role in the strongest sense of the term.

You work close to customers.

We believe customer proximity is a competitive advantage. Engineers talk directly to customers, join calls, and participate in tight feedback loops. Forward-deployed work is part of the job - not as support, but as the fastest path to product insight.

You shape what we build.

Engineers here don't wait for fully formed specs. If you see a problem worth solving, you're expected to make the case, design the experiment, and build the v0. Good product judgment is valued as highly as clean execution.

You ship across the stack.

You'll work wherever the problem is - backend systems processing TBs of internet-scale data, frontend workflows that make complex analysis feel intuitive, agent frameworks that automate what used to take weeks of manual work. Titles matter less than ownership.

You operate with clarity and focus.

Mondays we align. Fridays we retro. In between, you're trusted to prioritize what matters most - while staying sharp to signal from customers and the market.




THE TEAM

"The team you build is the company you build." — Vinod Khosla

You'll work alongside engineers from Palantir, Notion, Meta, Microsoft, Slack, and Datadog. Several have founded startups.

But credentials aren't the point.

This team cares deeply about craft, slope, and truth-seeking. People pair on hard problems, challenge each other in code review, and give direct, thoughtful feedback in 1:1s. Mentorship is intentional, not accidental - you'll learn faster here because the bar is high and the coaching is real.

We're building Outtake to be a long-term network of builders: people who go on to lead teams, start companies, and back each other over decades. The goal is simple - working here should make you meaningfully better.




YOU

This role tends to excite people who've built something from scratch and want to do it again - with better tools, sharper teammates, and real distribution.

You might be a fit if:

 - You've shipped products that users actually used - and at least one that failed, and you understand why that mattered

 - You ramp quickly, thrive in ambiguity, and have a strong bias to action

 - You care deeply about why something is being built, not just how

 - You understand what success looks like for the customer and the business, not just the code

 - You've done some form of forward-deployed or customer-embedded engineering and found it energizing

If you need a roadmap handed to you, this won't be a fit.

If you want to help write the roadmap, you'll feel at home.

Bonus if you've:

 - Founded something (even if it didn't work)

 - Worked in forward-deployed or customer-embedded roles

 - Built with agents, LLMs, or large-scale data systems




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

What will you have shipped by month 6 that customers are asking about by name?

The engineers who thrive here tend to find a problem early - something they heard on a customer call, noticed in the data, or couldn't stop thinking about - and make it theirs. Within a few months, they've bui

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (Platform)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.


THE ROLE

This is a platform engineering role.

You shape how other engineers build.
You will own the foundations that product engineers rely on every day. You’ll design and maintain the systems, tools, and primitives that determine how fast, safely, and confidently the team can ship. Your work directly influences developer workflows, reliability, and day-to-day engineering velocity.

You optimize for leverage.
You will prioritize work that multiplies impact across the engineering org. Instead of solving the same problem repeatedly, you’ll turn patterns into platforms and one-off fixes into systems. The goal is to make it easier for engineers to move quickly and do the right thing by default.

You envision and build durable foundations.
You will develop and evolve a point of view on how engineers should work, then translate that vision into code and systems. You’ll operate comfortably in messy, fast-moving environments and steadily turn them into something more solid without slowing the team down. This role expects you to balance long-term thinking with practical execution.




THE TEAM

"The team you build is the company you build." — Vinod Khosla

Outtake is built by former founders and engineers from Palantir, Notion, Meta, Slack, and Datadog - people who’ve shipped real systems, made hard calls, and learned the cost of getting things wrong.

We care deeply about craft, slope, and truth-seeking. Work here is collaborative and demanding: people pair on hard problems, challenge each other in review, and give direct, thoughtful feedback. Ownership is real - individuals own problems end-to-end, from framing and decisions to execution and outcomes. Mentorship is intentional, not accidental; people take your growth seriously and expect the same from you. The drive is high: people here care a lot, push hard, and move fast, not because they’re told to, but because they want to.

We’re building Outtake as a long-term network of builders. The goal is simple - time spent here should compound. You should leave meaningfully better than when you arrived.




YOU

This role tends to excite people who like building the foundations of messy systems and get energy from enabling other engineers to do their best work.

You might be a fit if:

 - You’ve built and owned foundational systems that other engineers depended on, and you’ve lived with the consequences of those decisions

 - You ramp quickly, thrive in ambiguity, and don’t need problems fully specified before you start making progress

 - You understand the tension between ideal solutions and velocity, and know when to pursue one over the other

 - You instinctively look for leverage and prefer systemic fixes over one-off solutions

 - You have a point of view on how engineers should work, and you’re opinionated about turning that into code, tooling, and defaults

 - You’ve worked closely with other engineers or teams as an enabler, not just as an implementer

If you need a tightly defined scope and a fixed roadmap, this won’t be a fit.

If you want to help define and build the platform’s direction you’ll feel at home.

Bo

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Security Engineering
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.


ABOUT YOU

Engineering at Outtake is built on ownership, clarity, and end-to-end impact. We hire entrepreneurial engineers who think in systems, not tickets.

Our teams are flat and autonomous: engineers ship fast, make product decisions, and own outcomes from concept to deployment. We optimize for grit + velocity, which means every engineer operates with the scope, influence, and equity of a future CTO.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - You'll be our second security hire, which means massive ownership, the ability to shape the function from the ground up, and a direct line to how security scales with the company

 - Own the security posture of our cloud infrastructure: AWS, EKS, Vercel, Supabase

 - Build our detection and response capabilities for capable incident response

 - Design and operate our secrets, credential, and access management architecture across our services

 - Partner with engineering on secure-by-default patterns: IAM, network segmentation, privilege, and access control.

 - Help shape our agentic AI security posture as the platform evolves


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years in infrastructure security, security operations, or platform engineering with strong security ownership

 - Hands-on Kubernetes security: RBAC, network policies, admission control, secrets handling

 - Experience architecting effective precise D&R SIEM/SOAR systems

 - Comfort writing and shipping code in your language of choice

 - Real incident response experience as an IC, including communicating with non-security stakeholders under pressure

 - Pragmatism about the tradeoffs between security, velocity, and developer experience at an early-stage company


NICE TO HAVE

 - Background defending or attacking AI/ML systems and agent infrastructure

 - Endpoint security operations (EDR/MDR)

 - You've been the security person at a small company before and know how the function evolves



LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Collaboration, speed, and clarity matter.

 - Health: 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision for employees.

 - Time Away: Flexible PTO. We trust adults to manage energy, not clock time.

 - Culture & Team: Annual company retreats and regular in-person events.

Outtake is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive team.

source_jobs.title: Head of Sales Enablement
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.

We are building a reputation as the place where modern, AI-enabled enterprise selling is innovated, taught, and practiced. We have a strong foundation: a structured onboarding program, a living sales playbook, a deal inspection framework, and a certification system that produces ramp-ready AEs. Now we need someone to own it, elevate it, and make it a durable competitive advantage.

The Sales Enablement Leader will report directly to the VP of Global Sales and sit at the intersection of sales, marketing, and product. You use AI to enhance your work rather than do your work. You build systems. You love making salespeople better by helping them build real value for customers. And you want to do it at a company with category-defining technology and serious growth ambition.



WHAT YOU'LL NEED TO BRING

 - 5+ years in sales enablement, sales operations, or a closely related role at a B2B company

 - Proven track record building or running a structured AE onboarding program with measurable ramp outcomes — not just inheriting one

 - Ability to take a complex product or concept and turn it into clean, usable field material fast

 - Deep fluency with MEDDPICC or equivalent methodology, and the ability to coach reps on applying it in live deals

 - Experience in fast-moving, early-stage or high-growth environments where you built from scratch, not maintained

 - Genuine comfort with AI tools and a bias toward using them to make enablement more effective and scalable

 - Willingness to vibecode solutions when a problem needs a tool and one doesn't exist yet
   

WHAT YOU'LL ACHIEVE

 - Owning the full lifecycle of sales readiness: From Day 1 onboarding through ongoing field enablement for every AE at Outtake

 - Keeping the AE Sales Playbook, battle cards, demo scripts, and objection banks current with continual validation of effectiveness.

 - Building AI-powered workflows that reduce rep prep time, improve call quality, and accelerate deal velocity across the team

 - Partnering with Product Marketing to translate new messaging and positioning into field-ready content the moment it ships

 - Defining and tracking enablement metrics

 - Sitting at the table with Sales, Marketing, and Product as a peer, not a support function. Your work directly shapes how Outtake goes to market



LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Collaboration, speed, and clarity matter.

 - Health: 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision for employees.

 - Time Away: Flexible PTO. We trust adults to manage energy, not clock time.

 - Culture & Team: Annual company retreats and regular in-person events.

Outtake is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive team.

source_jobs.title: Head of Enterprise Sales, East
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT OUTTAKE

Outtake exists to empower and facilitate trust for a digital-first world. Today, impersonation, fraud, AI-driven scams, and identity abuse spread faster than any security team can respond to. So we built something different: an agentic AI platform that proactively detects, monitors, and takes down impersonators, automating protection in hours, not weeks.

 

Built by ex-Palantir, ex-CTO/founders, and ex-Notion engineers, Outtake is designed for clarity, autonomy, and velocity. Our goal is ambitious: become the trust layer of the modern internet. And we intend to do it with a lean, dense, exceptionally talented team.

 

Outtake is backed by top-tier investors and operators who believe in our mission and our model. With strong financial footing and a long runway, we prioritize creating an environment where people can do the best work of their careers.


THE ROLE

As Head of Enterprise Sales, East, you'll lead and grow Outtake's revenue presence across the Eastern US. You'll build and develop a team of AEs, and go deep with the CISOs, Threat Intel leaders, and brand protection executives who will define this market. You'll report directly to the VP, Global Sales and operate as a key voice shaping Outtake's go-to-market strategy.

At Outtake we are the company where AI Enabled Enterprise selling is innovated, taught, and practiced. Your successful programs and processes from the past are stepping off points to a more efficient AI driven sales process that will drive impactful customer outcomes.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Drive the number: Own and exceed quarterly and annual revenue targets for the Eastern US territory.

 - Lead from the front: Hiring, developing and leading a team of Enterprise Account Executives across the East.

 - Build territory strategy: Develop and execute a territory plan that balances pipeline coverage, strategic accounts, and vertical focus.

 - Command the executive room: Engage directly with CISOs, Threat Intelligence leads, fraud teams, and brand protection stakeholders at F500 and Global 2000 companies.

 - Drive expansion: Partner with Product, Marketing, and Customer Success to ensure customers achieve ROI and expand their footprint over time.

 - Run a tight operation: Build a cross-functional operating cadence around pipeline, forecasting, deal reviews, and regional go-to-market execution.

 - Be a regional presence: Actively represent Outtake in the East Coast cybersecurity and DRP community—at conferences, customer events, and key industry forums.

 - Shape the category: Feed strategic insights from the field directly into product roadmap and positioning conversations.

 


WHAT YOU'LL NEED TO BRING

 - 10+ years in enterprise B2B SaaS sales, with at least 5 years in a direct leadership role.

 - Proven track record closing six- and seven-figure deals with complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise buying cycles.

 - Background selling into security, risk, or adjacent enterprise technology. Comfort navigating CISO, SOC, and Threat Intel buyers is a strong advantage.

 - Experience building and developing sales teams from early-stage; you know how to hire, ramp, and retain high-performers.

 - Technical fluency sufficient to lead credible conversations with CISOs, Threat Intel teams, and brand protection leaders, and translate complex AI-driven capabilities into business value.

 - Hunter DNA. You create pipelines, experiment with new approaches, and push through ambiguity.

 - Presence in our Brooklyn office is required weekly.

 - Strong operational discipline: accurate forecasting, pipeline management, and structured deal review cadences.



LIFE AT OUTTAKE

 - Office: For those in NY, we are an in-person team (5 days a week, with flexibility as needed) working out of a stunning waterfront office in Brooklyn. Collaboration, speed, and clarity matter.

 - Health: 100% company-paid medical, dental, and vision for employees.

 - Time Away: Flexible PTO. We trust adults to manage energy, no
